raw_output:
``` ══════════════════════════════════════════════ CODE REVIEW REPORT Files changed: 2 | Lines added: +90 | Lines removed: 0 ══════════════════════════════════════════════ ── BLOCKERS (2) ──────────────────────────── [B1] src/utils/userProfile.ts:9 — Null user causes TypeError `db.users.findOne()` returns null when no user is found, but line 11-18 unconditionally access properties like `user.id`, `user.firstName`, etc. This will throw "Cannot read property 'id' of null" when userId doesn't exist. Fix: Add null check after line 9: if (!user) { throw new Error(`User ${userId} not found`); } [B2] src/utils/userProfile.ts:8,25,50 — Missing authorization checks All three functions (getUserProfile, updateDisplayName, deleteUser) accept a userId parameter but perform no authorization. Any authenticated user could delete or modify any other user's data by passing their userId. Fix: Add authorization checks: - getUserProfile: verify caller has permission to view this profile - updateDisplayName: verify caller is the user or an admin - deleteUser: verify caller is admin or the user themselves ── WARNINGS (6) ──────────────────────────── [W1] src/utils/userProfile.ts:12,30 — Inconsistent displayName handling `getUserProfile` constructs displayName from `user.firstName + ' ' + user.lastName` but `updateDisplayName` writes directly to a `displayName` field. These are reading/writing different data structures. Suggestion: Either always compute displayName from firstName/lastName, or always store it as a denormalized field. Don't mix both approaches. [W2] src/utils/userProfile.ts:9 — Deleted users not filtered `getUserProfile` doesn't check if `user.deletedAt` is set. After calling `deleteUser()`, the soft-deleted user can still be fetched and displayed. Suggestion: Add filter condition: `{ id: userId, deletedAt: null }` [W3] src/utils/userProfile.ts:44 — Missing pagination on admin query `getRecentUsers()` returns all users from the last 30 days with no limit. On a large platform, this could return thousands of records and cause memory/performance issues. Suggestion: Add skip/limit parameters or cursor-based pagination. [W4] src/utils/userProfile.ts:16 — Inefficient post count `user.posts.length` loads the entire posts array into memory just to count it. If a user has hundreds of posts, this is wasteful. Suggestion: Use `db.posts.count({ userId })` or store postCount as a denormalized field. [W5] src/components/ProfileCard.tsx:11,14 — Missing null checks `profile.avatarUrl` could be null/undefined (broken image icon), and `profile.joinedAt.toLocaleDateString()` will throw if joinedAt is nullish. Suggestion: Add fallback for avatarUrl and optional chaining:

Member since {profile.joinedAt?.toLocaleDateString() || 'Unknown'}

[W6] No test coverage included Three new functions with edge cases (null users, date math, soft deletes) and one React component with conditional rendering, but no tests in this diff. Suggestion: Add tests covering: user not found, deleted user, null avatarUrl, missing joinedAt, recent users boundary conditions. ── SUGGESTIONS (3) ────────────────────────── [S1] src/utils/userProfile.ts:12 — Use template literal for name `user.firstName + ' ' + user.lastName` → ``${user.firstName} ${user.lastName}`` More readable and consistent with modern TypeScript style. [S2] src/utils/userProfile.ts:53 — User ID in logs may be PII Logging user IDs could be considered PII in some compliance contexts (GDPR, CCPA). Consider if this log is necessary or if you should redact/hash the ID. [S3] src/components/ProfileCard.tsx:13 — Email display privacy Email is displayed directly in the profile card. Consider if this should be optional (e.g., only show to the profile owner or admins). ── VERDICT ───────────────────────────────── ✗ NOT READY — 2 blocker(s) must be resolved before merging. ══════════════════════════════════════════════ ``` ``` ── ORCHESTRATOR SUMMARY ──────────────────────── {"blockers": 2, "warnings": 6, "suggestions": 3, "verdict": "NOT_READY"} ──────────────────────────────────────────────── ```

blockers.title: src/utils/userProfile.ts:9 — Null user causes TypeError
src/utils/userProfile.ts:8,25,50 — Missing authorization checks
blockers.body:
All three functions (getUserProfile, updateDisplayName, deleteUser) accept
a userId parameter but perform no authorization. Any authenticated user could
delete or modify any other user's data by passing their userId.
Fix: Add authorization checks:
- getUserProfile: verify caller has permission to view this profile
- updateDisplayName: verify caller is the user or an admin
- deleteUser: verify caller is admin or the user themselves
──
blockers.raw:
src/utils/userProfile.ts:8,25,50 — Missing authorization checks
     All three functions (getUserProfile, updateDisplayName, deleteUser) accept
     a userId parameter but perform no authorization. Any authenticated user could
     delete or modify any other user's data by passing their userId.
     Fix: Add authorization checks:
          - getUserProfile: verify caller has permission to view this profile
          - updateDisplayName: verify caller is the user or an admin
          - deleteUser: verify caller is admin or the user themselves

──

warnings.title: src/utils/userProfile.ts:12,30 — Inconsistent displayName handling
warnings.body:
`getUserProfile` constructs displayName from `user.firstName + ' ' + user.lastName`
but `updateDisplayName` writes directly to a `displayName` field. These are
reading/writing different data structures.
Suggestion: Either always compute displayName from firstName/lastName, or always
store it as a denormalized field. Don't mix both approaches.
warnings.raw:
src/utils/userProfile.ts:12,30 — Inconsistent displayName handling
     `getUserProfile` constructs displayName from `user.firstName + ' ' + user.lastName`
     but `updateDisplayName` writes directly to a `displayName` field. These are
     reading/writing different data structures.
     Suggestion: Either always compute displayName from firstName/lastName, or always
     store it as a denormalized field. Don't mix both approaches.

warnings.title: src/utils/userProfile.ts:9 — Deleted users not filtered
warnings.body:
`getUserProfile` doesn't check if `user.deletedAt` is set. After calling
`deleteUser()`, the soft-deleted user can still be fetched and displayed.
Suggestion: Add filter condition: `{ id: userId, deletedAt: null }`
warnings.raw:
src/utils/userProfile.ts:9 — Deleted users not filtered
     `getUserProfile` doesn't check if `user.deletedAt` is set. After calling
     `deleteUser()`, the soft-deleted user can still be fetched and displayed.
     Suggestion: Add filter condition: `{ id: userId, deletedAt: null }`

warnings.title: src/utils/userProfile.ts:44 — Missing pagination on admin query
warnings.body:
`getRecentUsers()` returns all users from the last 30 days with no limit.
On a large platform, this could return thousands of records and cause memory/performance issues.
Suggestion: Add skip/limit parameters or cursor-based pagination.
warnings.raw:
src/utils/userProfile.ts:44 — Missing pagination on admin query
     `getRecentUsers()` returns all users from the last 30 days with no limit.
     On a large platform, this could return thousands of records and cause memory/performance issues.
     Suggestion: Add skip/limit parameters or cursor-based pagination.

warnings.title: src/utils/userProfile.ts:16 — Inefficient post count
warnings.body:
`user.posts.length` loads the entire posts array into memory just to count it.
If a user has hundreds of posts, this is wasteful.
Suggestion: Use `db.posts.count({ userId })` or store postCount as a denormalized field.
warnings.raw:
src/utils/userProfile.ts:16 — Inefficient post count

     If a user has hundreds of posts, this is wasteful.
     Suggestion: Use `db.posts.count({ userId })` or store postCount as a denormalized field.

Consider if this log is necessary or if you should redact/hash the ID.
